title: Restructure ALCS Directory: move Blackrock + Centrifuge to Completed, align Active with ICD numbering
body:
## Summary

LDR's review feedback identified the original collision (Paxos + Anchorage at .14) as a symptom of a deeper structural issue: the Active Instances Directory at `A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.1.2.1` still listed Blackrock (.6) and Centrifuge (.7), but their ICDs had already been moved to the Completed Instances tree at `A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.4`. The 2-position offset between Active Directory and ICDs was the root cause of the collision and the broader Directoryâ†”ICD numbering mismatch (e.g. Anchorage Directory said .14, ICD said .13).

This commit replaces the original `.14 â†’ .15` collision patch with a structural fix per LDR's suggestion: move the stale Directory entries to the (previously empty) Completed Instances Directory, and renumber the remaining Active entries to close the gap. Result: Active Directory and ICD numbering align cleanly (Anchorage is .13 in both); Completed Directory now mirrors the populated Completed ICDs.

## Changes

**Move to Completed Instances Directory** (`A.6.1.1.1.2.6.1.1.3`):
- Blackrock: `.2.1.6` â†’ `.3.1` (UUID preserved)
- Blackrock USDC ICD Location: `.2.1.6.1` â†’ `.3.1.1` (UUID preserved)
- Centrifuge: `.2.1.7` â†’ `.3.2` (UUID preserved)
- Centrifuge USDC ICD Location: `.2.1.7.1` â†’ `.3.2.1` (UUID preserved)

**Renumber Active Directory** (Ethereum Mainnet, decrement by 2):
- Superstate `.8` â†’ `.6` (1 child)
- Curve `.9` â†’ `.7` (4 children)
- Morpho `.10` â†’ `.8` (4 children)
- Spark Savings V2 `.11` â†’ `.9` (4 children)
- Arkis `.12` â†’ `.10` (1 child)
- Uniswap v4 `.13` â†’ `.11` (2 children)
- Paxos `.14` â†’ `.12` (4 children)
- Anchorage `.15` â†’ `.13` (2 children) â€” naturally aligns with ICD `.13`, no patch needed

## Verification

- All UUIDs preserved
- Link bodies in moved Blackrock + Centrifuge entries already pointed to Completed ICDs (no link changes needed)
- Diff is +46/-46 lines: pure renumbering, no content changes
- Validator passes
- No doc_no string references found elsewhere in the Atlas (all references are UUID-linked)

## Note on structure

Skipping the network layer (no `.3.1 = Ethereum Mainnet` parent under Completed Instances Directory) is a minimum-viable choice for this correction: it keeps the diff to pure doc_no renumbering of existing atoms with no new documents created. Adding a network layer would require creating new parent atoms and restructuring, which is out of scope for what is fundamentally a numbering correction.

Worth considering as a follow-up: whether to add a network layer to both Completed Instances Directory and Completed Instances trees, for symmetry with the Active Directory's network-layered structure (`.2.1 = Ethereum Mainnet`). That would be a deliberate structural decision rather than a side-effect of fixing this discrepancy.
author: adamgfraser
